fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Washington Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Washington - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity washington, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to washington, pharmacy technician compact washington, washington pharmacy technician endorsement application

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years, on or before your birthday

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Washington.

steps.title: Meet the Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: You must be 18 years of age, have a high school diploma or equivalent, and have good moral character.

steps.title: Complete a Training Program
steps.description: Complete a Board-approved pharmacy technician training program or employer-based training program.

steps.title: Register for a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) Account
steps.description: Go to secureaccess.wa.gov and create your SAW account. You'll set your own user ID and password. Check your email and click the activation link to complete registration. If you have issues, contact WATech at 855-928-3241.

steps.title: Link to HELMS Through SAW
steps.description: Log into SAW, select 'Add a New Service,' choose 'Department of Health,' then scroll to 'Health Professional and Facility Licensing (HELMS)' and select 'Apply.' Complete the identity verification questions using your full legal name.

steps.title: Submit Your Application in HELMS
steps.description: From MyServices, open HELMS, search for Pharmacy Technician, and click 'Start a New Application.' Complete all required fields. Note: after 14 days of inactivity your application is deleted — log back in to keep it active.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the $140 credential fee plus a 2.5% convenience fee online. Payment must be in U.S. funds from a U.S. financial institution. Ensure your billing address matches what your bank has on file.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Washington offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Washington by submitting a new application to the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Washington-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Washington pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The Washington Department of Health lists a $140 original pharmacy technician credential fee and a $140 renewal fee.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Washington have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Washington. To work in Washington, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Washington?
faqs.answer: Washington does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline on its licensing information page. Use the board application tools and provider search to check status.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Washington while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Contact the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Washington.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Washington by submitting a new application to the Washington Pharmacy Quality Assurance Commission. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Washington-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Washington
hero.dek: Apply online through the Washington Department of Health HELMS portal (secureaccess.wa.gov). You'll need to create a SecureAccess Washington (SAW) account first, then link to HELMS to start your application.
